expectations.text: Response uses useMutation with mutationFn for PATCH call
expectations.evidence: useMutation is used with mutationFn that performs a PATCH call: "mutationFn: async ({ taskId, status }: UpdateTaskStatusInput): Promise<Task> => { const response = await fetch(`/api/tasks/${taskId}`, { method: 'PATCH', ... }); }".

expectations.text: Response implements onMutate with cache snapshot and optimistic update
expectations.evidence: onMutate snapshots previous data ("const previousTasks = queryClient.getQueryData<Task[]>(queryKeys.tasks.all);") and optimistically updates the cache ("queryClient.setQueryData<Task[]>(queryKeys.tasks.all, (old) => { ... return old.map((task) => task.id === taskId ? { ...task, status } : task); });").

expectations.text: Response implements onError with rollback from context
expectations.evidence: onError restores from context: "onError: (_err, _variables, context) => { if (context?.previousTasks) { queryClient.setQueryData<Task[]>(queryKeys.tasks.all, context.previousTasks); } }".
onSettled invalidates queries: "onSettled: () => { queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: queryKeys.tasks.all }); }".

expectations.text: Response cancels outgoing queries before optimistic update
expectations.evidence: First step in onMutate: "await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: queryKeys.tasks.all });" with comment '1. Cancel outgoing refetches so they don't overwrite our optimistic update'.
